page: Amazon Web Services m6id.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and the baseline network bandwidth is 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the m6id.8xlarge in the average range for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1890.69 and a multi-core score of 30861.82. Memory bandwidth tests show 1658.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 149.49 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the server handles 31.25 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 14804.52 on PassMark database tests. For language model workloads, it demonstrates strong text generation performance of 270.9 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, but drops to a weak 6.14 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

The inclusion of 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the added cost of external block storage. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy machine learning training, as evidenced by the weak performance on larger LLM inference tasks. The server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network connection. Benchmark data shows average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 14183.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 36587.82. While it demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 270.9 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, performance drops significantly on larger 70B models due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is optimized for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced compute and high-speed local storage performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD for balanced general-purpose workloads and database operations.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated high-speed NVMe local instance storage.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor.
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor.
Strong text generation performance of 270.9 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
